title: Combine staticcall and result loading using mload(staticcall(...)) trick
description: staticcall returns 1 on success, 0 on failure. By writing the output to memory offset 0x01 and using result := mload(staticcall(...)), you get mload(1) on success — which reads from offset 0x01 where the precompile just wrote the result. This combines the call and result extraction into one expression, saving a local variable assignment.
pattern_before:
let success := staticcall(gas(), 2, 0x00, 0x20, 0x00, 0x20)
let result := mload(0x00)
pattern_after:
// Output written to 0x01; staticcall returns 1 on success → mload(1) reads from 0x01
result := mload(staticcall(gas(), 2, 0x00, 0x20, 0x01, 0x20))
when_to_apply: Precompile calls with 32-byte output where the success return value (1) can double as the output memory offset
when_not_to_apply: Regular contract calls where the output position doesn't happen to be 0x01, or when you need to check for failure separately
